description: Service Catalog Frontend - Angular UI for managing services and categories with foundational HTTP setup, NgRx SignalStore state management, and full CRUD UI components

userStories.title: Fix ESLint prefix and add HttpClient provider
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the ESLint prefix fixed to 'chairly' and HttpClient configured so that the frontend can make API calls.
acceptanceCriteria: libs/chairly/eslint.config.mjs component selector prefix changed from 'lib' to 'chairly' (kebab-case, element)
libs/chairly/eslint.config.mjs directive selector prefix changed from 'lib' to 'chairly' (camelCase, attribute)
libs/shared/eslint.config.mjs component selector prefix changed from 'lib' to 'chairly' (kebab-case, element)
libs/shared/eslint.config.mjs directive selector prefix changed from 'lib' to 'chairly' (camelCase, attribute)
provideHttpClient() added to the providers array in apps/chairly/src/app/app.config.ts
import { provideHttpClient } from '@angular/common/http' is present in app.config.ts
npx nx affected -t lint --base=main passes
npx nx affected -t test --base=main passes
npx nx affected -t build --base=main passes
userStories.notes: The lib ESLint configs currently use 'lib' as the prefix but CLAUDE.md requires 'chairly'. Fix both libs. HttpClient is needed for all API services in later stories.

userStories.title: API proxy config and environment setup
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a dev proxy for /api calls and an API_BASE_URL injection token so that the frontend can communicate with the backend.
acceptanceCriteria: proxy.conf.json created in apps/chairly/ with '/api' target 'http://localhost:5205' and changeOrigin: true
apps/chairly/project.json serve target updated with proxyConfig pointing to apps/chairly/proxy.conf.json
API_BASE_URL InjectionToken<string> created in libs/shared/src/lib/util/ with a sensible default value of '/api'
The injection token is exported from libs/shared/src/index.ts via the shared barrel export
npx nx affected -t lint --base=main passes
npx nx affected -t test --base=main passes
npx nx affected -t build --base=main passes
userStories.notes: The backend runs on localhost:5205. The proxy rewrites /api/* to the backend. The injection token allows services to use inject(API_BASE_URL) for the base URL. Export through the shared lib barrel.

userStories.title: TypeScript interfaces for service catalog DTOs
userStories.description: As a developer, I need TypeScript interfaces matching the backend DTOs so that API responses are type-safe.
acceptanceCriteria: ServiceResponse interface in libs/chairly/src/lib/services/util/ with fields: id (string), name (string), description (string | null), duration (string — TimeSpan format HH:MM:SS), price (number), categoryId (string | null), categoryName (string | null), isActive (boolean), sortOrder (number), createdAtUtc (string), createdBy (string), updatedAtUtc (string | null), updatedBy (string | null)
CreateServiceRequest interface with fields: name (string), description (string | null), duration (string), price (number), categoryId (string | null), sortOrder (number)
UpdateServiceRequest interface with same fields as CreateServiceRequest
ServiceCategoryResponse interface with fields: id (string), name (string), sortOrder (number), createdAtUtc (string), createdBy (string)
CreateServiceCategoryRequest interface with fields: name (string), sortOrder (number)
UpdateServiceCategoryRequest interface with same fields as CreateServiceCategoryRequest
All interfaces exported from a barrel file in services/util/
npx nx affected -t lint --base=main passes
npx nx affected -t build --base=main passes
userStories.notes: GUIDs from the backend map to string in TypeScript. TimeSpan maps to string in format 'HH:MM:SS'. DateTimeOffset maps to string (ISO 8601). These interfaces must exactly match the backend response DTOs from ServiceResponse.cs and ServiceCategoryResponse.cs.

userStories.title: ServiceCategoryApiService with unit tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an HTTP service for category CRUD operations so that the store can call the backend.
acceptanceCriteria: ServiceCategoryApiService created in libs/chairly/src/lib/services/data-access/
Uses inject(HttpClient) and inject(API_BASE_URL) from shared util
getAll() method: GET /api/service-categories, returns Observable<ServiceCategoryResponse[]>
create(request: CreateServiceCategoryRequest) method: POST /api/service-categories, returns Observable<ServiceCategoryResponse>
update(id: string, request: UpdateServiceCategoryRequest) method: PUT /api/service-categories/{id}, returns Observable<ServiceCategoryResponse>
delete(id: string) method: DELETE /api/service-categories/{id}, returns Observable<void>
All methods have explicit return types
Unit tests using HttpClientTestingModule (or provideHttpClientTesting) verify each method makes the correct HTTP call with correct URL, method, and body
npx nx affected -t lint --base=main passes
npx nx affected -t test --base=main passes
npx nx affected -t build --base=main passes
userStories.notes: Use Angular's HttpClient. Base URL from the API_BASE_URL injection token. Test with HttpTestingController to verify requests. Service should be providedIn: 'root' or provided at the route level — choose based on existing patterns.

userStories.title: ServiceCategoryStore (NgRx SignalStore) with unit tests

State shape: categories (ServiceCategoryResponse[]), isLoading (boolean), error (string | null)
loadCategories method: sets isLoading, calls API getAll(), updates categories on success, sets error on failure
createCategory method: calls API create(), adds result to categories array
updateCategory method: calls API update(), replaces the category in the array
deleteCategory method: calls API delete(), removes from array
Unit tests for all store methods using a mocked ServiceCategoryApiService
Tests verify state transitions (isLoading, categories updated, error set on failure)
npx nx affected -t lint --base=main passes
npx nx affected -t test --base=main passes
npx nx affected -t build --base=main passes
userStories.notes: Use @ngrx/signals signalStore with withState, withMethods, and withComputed as needed. Use rxMethod or patchState for async operations. The store will be provided at the route level in SCF-014.

userStories.title: ServiceStore (NgRx SignalStore) with unit tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a SignalStore for services so that UI components can reactively consume service state.
acceptanceCriteria: ServiceStore created in libs/chairly/src/lib/services/data-access/ using signalStore()
State shape: services (ServiceResponse[]), isLoading (boolean), error (string | null)
Computed signal: activeServices — filters services where isActive === true
Computed signal: servicesByCategory — groups services by categoryId (Record<string, ServiceResponse[]>) with a 'uncategorized' key for null categoryId
loadServices method: sets isLoading, calls API getAll(), updates services on success, sets error on failure
createService method: calls API create(), adds result to services array
updateService method: calls API update(), replaces the service in the array
deleteService method: calls API delete(), removes from array
toggleActive method: calls API toggleActive(), replaces the service in the array with the toggled version
Unit tests for all store methods and computed signals
npx nx affected -t lint --base=main passes
npx nx affected -t test --base=main passes
npx nx affected -t build --base=main passes
userStories.notes: Same patterns as SCF-006. The computed signals derive from the services array. servicesByCategory groups by categoryId, using 'uncategorized' as the key when categoryId is null.

OnPush change detection, standalone component
Component selector: chairly-confirmation-dialog
Exported from libs/shared/src/index.ts
Unit test verifies dialog opens, emits confirmed on confirm click, emits cancelled on cancel click
npx nx affected -t lint --base=main passes
npx nx affected -t test --base=main passes
npx nx affected -t build --base=main passes

userStories.title: DurationPipe and helper functions with unit tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I need duration conversion helpers so that .NET TimeSpan values can be displayed as human-readable minutes.
acceptanceCriteria: parseDuration(timeSpan: string): number function in libs/chairly/src/lib/services/util/ — converts 'HH:MM:SS' to total minutes (e.g. '01:30:00' → 90)
formatDurationToTimeSpan(minutes: number): string function — converts minutes to 'HH:MM:SS' (e.g. 90 → '01:30:00')
DurationPipe (name: 'duration') in libs/chairly/src/lib/services/util/ — uses parseDuration to transform TimeSpan string to 'X min' or 'Xh Ymin' display format
Unit tests for parseDuration with edge cases: '00:00:00' → 0, '00:45:00' → 45, '02:00:00' → 120
Unit tests for formatDurationToTimeSpan with edge cases: 0 → '00:00:00', 60 → '01:00:00'
Unit tests for DurationPipe transform output
npx nx affected -t lint --base=main passes
npx nx affected -t test --base=main passes
npx nx affected -t build --base=main passes
userStories.notes: The backend uses .NET TimeSpan serialized as 'HH:MM:SS'. The frontend needs to convert this to minutes for display and form inputs, and back to TimeSpan format for API requests. The pipe is for template display only.

Injects ServiceStore and ServiceCategoryStore
On init, calls loadServices() and loadCategories() on both stores

Add Service button in the header opens ServiceFormDialogComponent in create mode

Delete action from table opens ConfirmationDialogComponent with isDestructive=true, on confirm calls store.deleteService()
Toggle active action from table calls store.toggleActive()
Category panel events wired to ServiceCategoryStore methods (create, update, delete with confirmation)

OnPush change detection, standalone component, selector: chairly-service-list-page
npx nx affected -t lint --base=main passes
npx nx affected -t test --base=main passes
npx nx affected -t build --base=main passes
userStories.notes: This is the smart container that orchestrates everything. It connects stores to presentational components. Use viewChild() (signal-based) to get references to dialog components for open/close. Layout should be responsive — sidebar collapses on small screens or stacks below.

userStories.title: Fix CI: nx affected PR step fails due to missing main branch ref
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the GitHub Actions frontend CI workflow to correctly resolve the main branch so that nx affected works on pull requests.
acceptanceCriteria: In .github/workflows/frontend-ci.yml, the 'Run affected tasks (PR)' step is changed to: npx nx affected -t lint test build e2e --base=origin/main --head=HEAD

In .github/workflows/frontend-ci.yml, the 'Run affected tasks (push)' step is changed to: npx nx affected -t lint test build e2e --base=origin/main~1 --head=HEAD (already uses origin/ prefix, verify it is consistent)
A 'Fetch base branch' step is added BEFORE the affected tasks steps: run: git fetch origin main
The workflow no longer fails with 'fatal: ambiguous argument main'
npx nx affected -t lint test build --base=origin/main passes locally
userStories.notes: The root cause: actions/checkout with fetch-depth: 0 fetches all history but does NOT create a local tracking ref for 'main'. nx affected --base=main then fails. The fix: add 'git fetch origin main' step to create the remote-tracking ref, or use origin/main instead of main as the base. Both changes together are safest. The push step already says --base=origin/main~1 and is fine; only the PR step uses the bare 'main' ref.

The model files are: service.models.ts (ServiceResponse, CreateServiceRequest, UpdateServiceRequest) and service-category.models.ts (ServiceCategoryResponse, CreateServiceCategoryRequest, UpdateServiceCategoryRequest). The util/ folder should only contain pure utility functions (parseDuration, formatDurationToTimeSpan) after this change. Update all barrel files accordingly.

userStories.title: Move services.routes.ts from feature/ to services domain root
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the route configuration file at the root of the services domain (not inside feature/) so that it is accessible without being buried inside a UI layer folder.
acceptanceCriteria: libs/chairly/src/lib/services/services.routes.ts created (file moved from feature/services.routes.ts)
libs/chairly/src/lib/services/feature/services.routes.ts deleted

app.routes.ts still works: import('@org/chairly-lib').then(m => m.servicesRoutes) resolves correctly

userStories.notes: The services.routes.ts file logically belongs at the domain root because it is the entry point for the services feature, not a feature component itself. After moving, the relative imports inside services.routes.ts will need updating. The file currently imports from '../data-access' and './service-list-page.component' — from the domain root these become './data-access' and './feature/service-list-page.component'. Note: story SCF-020 will move service-list-page.component into a subfolder, so coordinate import path — use the feature/index.ts barrel (import from './feature') to avoid coupling to internal structure.

acceptanceCriteria: New folder created: libs/chairly/src/lib/services/feature/service-list-page/

Currently only ServiceListPageComponent exists in feature/. Move it to feature/service-list-page/. If additional feature components are added in future, they each get their own subfolder (e.g., feature/service-detail/). The feature/index.ts barrel hides the internal subfolder structure from consumers.

userStories.title: Extract all inline component templates to separate .html files and enforce via ESLint
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all Angular component templates in dedicated .html files (not inline) so that templates are separately navigable, diffable, and clearly separated from component logic.
acceptanceCriteria: ESLint rule added to root eslint.config.mjs in the Angular TS rules section: '@angular-eslint/component-max-inline-declarations': ['error', { 'template': 0 }]
ServiceListPageComponent: template extracted to feature/service-list-page/service-list-page.component.html, component uses templateUrl: './service-list-page.component.html'
CategoryPanelComponent: template extracted to ui/category-panel.component.html, component uses templateUrl: './category-panel.component.html'
ServiceFormDialogComponent: template extracted to ui/service-form-dialog.component.html, component uses templateUrl: './service-form-dialog.component.html'
ServiceTableComponent: template extracted to ui/service-table.component.html, component uses templateUrl: './service-table.component.html'
ConfirmationDialogComponent (in shared/ui/): template extracted to ui/confirmation-dialog.component.html, component uses templateUrl: './confirmation-dialog.component.html'
No component in the codebase uses inline 'template:' property — only 'templateUrl:' is used
All extracted .html files contain valid Angular template syntax (same as the inline template, exactly)
Unit tests for all components still pass (test setup uses TestBed which resolves templateUrl correctly with Vite)
npx nx affected -t lint test build --base=origin/main passes
userStories.notes: This story must be implemented AFTER SCF-020 (feature subfolders) so that ServiceListPageComponent is already in its subfolder before its .html file is created there. The ESLint rule '@angular-eslint/component-max-inline-declarations' with { 'template': 0 } makes inline templates a lint error. When extracting templates: (1) remove the 'template:' property from the @Component decorator, (2) add 'templateUrl:' pointing to the .html file, (3) create the .html file with the exact same content as the inline template. The Angular/Vite test setup in this project resolves templateUrl correctly — no special test configuration needed.

acceptanceCriteria: libs/chairly/src/lib/services/data-access/.gitkeep deleted (folder has real files)

Check each folder listed above before deleting — if for some reason a folder is empty (after structural refactors in earlier stories), keep the .gitkeep.

userStories.title: Add Playwright e2e tests for service catalog
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Playwright e2e tests for the service catalog page so that basic UI functionality is verified end-to-end.
acceptanceCriteria: apps/chairly-e2e/src/example.spec.ts updated: the 'has title' test now navigates to '/' and expects the h1 to contain 'Services' (not 'Welcome', since the app now redirects to /services)
apps/chairly-e2e/src/service-catalog.spec.ts created with at least the following tests:
- 'navigates to /services and shows the page header': navigates to '/services', expects page.locator('h1').innerText() to contain 'Services'
- 'shows the Add Service button': expects page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Add Service' }) to be visible
- 'opens the add service dialog when Add Service is clicked': clicks Add Service button, expects a dialog to be visible
- 'closes the add service dialog when Cancel is clicked': opens dialog, clicks Cancel, expects dialog to be hidden
- 'shows the Categories panel': expects the text 'Categories' to be visible on the page
All e2e tests pass when run with npx nx e2e chairly-e2e (app served without backend — tests do not depend on API responses, only on UI structure)
npx nx affected -t lint test build e2e --base=origin/main passes
userStories.notes: The e2e tests run against the Angular app served without a backend. Since the stores make HTTP calls on init (loadServices, loadCategories), these will fail silently — the page will show empty states, not errors, as the stores set isLoading=false on error. The e2e tests should test UI structure and interactions, NOT API-dependent content (don't assert on table rows or category items since there's no backend). Use @playwright/test. The playwright.config.ts already exists at apps/chairly-e2e/playwright.config.ts — check the webServer config to understand how the app is served. The test for the dialog should use page.locator('dialog') or similar — the components use native <dialog> element.

userStories.title: Update CLAUDE.md with new folder conventions and update Ralph stop condition to check CI
userStories.description: As a developer, I want CLAUDE.md updated with the new folder structure conventions and Ralph's stop condition updated to verify CI passes before signaling completion.
acceptanceCriteria: Root CLAUDE.md Frontend Architecture section updated to document the services domain folder structure: models/ (interfaces and types), pipes/ (Angular pipes), util/ (pure utility functions), feature/{feature-name}/ (each feature gets its own subfolder), and routes file at domain root

Root CLAUDE.md Code Conventions — Frontend section updated with: 'Each domain layer has dedicated subfolders: models/ for interfaces, pipes/ for Angular pipes, util/ for pure functions. Routes file lives at domain root, not inside feature/. Each smart component inside feature/ has its own subfolder.'

Root CLAUDE.md Code Conventions — Frontend section updated with: 'Component templates must always be in separate .html files (templateUrl:). Inline templates (template:) are forbidden and enforced by ESLint.'

Root CLAUDE.md Working Method — Autonomous/Headless Mode section updated: after pushing and creating the PR, add a step to verify CI passes: 'After creating the PR, verify CI passes: run gh run watch --exit-status to wait for the pipeline. If it fails, investigate and fix before signaling COMPLETE.'

scripts/ralph/CLAUDE.md Stop Condition section updated to include: after creating the PR with git push + gh pr create, run 'gh run watch --exit-status' to wait for CI. Only signal COMPLETE if CI passes. If CI fails, investigate the failure, fix it, push a new commit, and wait again.
npx nx affected -t lint test build --base=origin/main passes (CLAUDE.md changes don't affect lint/test/build, but verify no regressions)
userStories.notes: This story updates documentation only (CLAUDE.md files) plus the Ralph agent instruction. The key change to Ralph's behavior: instead of creating PR and immediately signaling COMPLETE, Ralph should wait for CI to pass. Use 'gh run watch --exit-status' which exits 0 if CI passes, non-zero if it fails. If CI fails, Ralph should read the failure logs with 'gh run view --log-failed' and fix the issue. The folder convention changes (models/, pipes/, feature subfolder, templateUrl) should be documented so future Ralph iterations follow them without needing to be told.

userStories.title: Fix Sass @import deprecation: separate Tailwind CSS entry file from styles.scss
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the Sass @import deprecation warning eliminated so that the build output is clean and only warns about real issues.
acceptanceCriteria: apps/chairly/src/tailwind.css created with a single line: @import 'tailwindcss';
apps/chairly/src/styles.scss updated to remove the @import 'tailwindcss'; line — the file should be empty (a comment explaining its purpose is fine)
apps/chairly/project.json build.options.styles array updated to: ["apps/chairly/src/tailwind.css", "apps/chairly/src/styles.scss"] — tailwind.css listed first
npx nx build chairly produces no Sass deprecation warning about @import rules
Tailwind utility classes still apply correctly in the built output
npx nx affected -t lint test build --base=origin/main passes
userStories.notes: Root cause: styles.scss uses @import 'tailwindcss' which Dart Sass intercepts before PostCSS gets a chance to process it, triggering the 'Sass @import rules are deprecated' warning. The fix: move the Tailwind import to a plain .css file. Angular's build pipeline processes .css global style files with PostCSS directly, bypassing Sass. The styles.scss is kept as the SCSS entry point for any future SCSS-specific global styles (custom variables, mixins, font imports) but must NEVER contain @import for CSS libraries. Order in the styles array matters: tailwind.css first so Tailwind base styles load before any custom overrides in styles.scss.

userStories.title: Update CLAUDE.md to document Tailwind/SCSS separation convention
userStories.description: As a developer, I want CLAUDE.md updated with the Tailwind + SCSS separation convention so that future implementations never put CSS library imports inside .scss files.
acceptanceCriteria: Root CLAUDE.md Frontend Architecture section updated: document that the global styles setup consists of two files — tailwind.css (Tailwind v4 @import, processed by PostCSS only, never through Sass) and styles.scss (SCSS-specific global styles: variables, mixins, font declarations)
Root CLAUDE.md Forbidden section updated: add entry 'No @import of CSS libraries (e.g. Tailwind) inside .scss files — use a separate plain .css entry file instead'

Root CLAUDE.md Code Conventions — Frontend section notes: 'Tailwind v4 is imported in apps/chairly/src/tailwind.css (plain CSS). SCSS global styles go in apps/chairly/src/styles.scss. These two files must never be merged.'
npx nx affected -t lint test build --base=origin/main passes
userStories.notes: Documentation-only story. The pattern to document: Tailwind v4 uses PostCSS for @import 'tailwindcss' processing. When this import is placed in a .scss file, Dart Sass intercepts it first and emits a deprecation warning because Sass @import is deprecated in Dart Sass 3.x. The structural fix (done in SCF-025) is to keep the Tailwind import in a plain .css file. This story documents that decision in CLAUDE.md so future Ralph iterations and developers never merge these concerns again. Add to the Forbidden section so it is unambiguous.

userStories.title: Remove manual sortOrder from forms; implement drag & drop reordering
userStories.description: As a user, I do not want to manually enter a sort order number. Instead I want to reorder services and categories by dragging and dropping rows/items in the list.
acceptanceCriteria: ServiceFormDialogComponent: sortOrder field removed from FormGroup and from the template. The category select now spans the full row (no sort-order sibling column).

ServiceListPageComponent.onServiceSaved: for create, inject sortOrder as this.services().length before calling store.createService(); for update, inject sortOrder as this.selectedService()!.sortOrder to preserve the existing value.

CategoryPanelComponent: sortOrder field removed from addForm and editForm. Both FormGroups no longer contain sortOrder. Both sortOrder inputs removed from the template. The 'Order: {{ category.sortOrder }}' line removed from the category list display.
CategoryPanelComponent.saveNewCategory: sets sortOrder = this.categories().length on the emitted CreateServiceCategoryRequest.
CategoryPanelComponent.saveEdit: preserves the existing category.sortOrder on the emitted UpdateServiceCategoryRequest.

CategoryPanelComponent: list items are draggable (HTML5 drag & drop, no third-party library). Each <li> has draggable='true', handles (dragstart), (dragover), (drop). On drop, the categories array is reordered in memory and a new categoriesReordered OutputEmitterRef<ServiceCategoryResponse[]> event is emitted with the reordered array.

ServiceTableComponent: table rows are draggable (HTML5 drag & drop). Each <tr> has draggable='true', handles (dragstart), (dragover), (drop). On drop, a new servicesReordered OutputEmitterRef<ServiceResponse[]> event is emitted with the reordered array.

ServiceCategoryStore: new reorderCategories(ordered: ServiceCategoryResponse[]) method. Updates local state immediately (optimistic update: replaces categories with the ordered array with sortOrder set to index). Then calls apiService.update(id, { name, sortOrder: index }) for each category in order.

ServiceStore: new reorderServices(ordered: ServiceResponse[]) method. Updates local state immediately. Then calls apiService.update(id, { name, description, duration, price, categoryId, sortOrder: index }) for each service in order.

ServiceListPageComponent: wires (categoriesReordered) from CategoryPanelComponent to categoryStore.reorderCategories($event). Wires (servicesReordered) from ServiceTableComponent to serviceStore.reorderServices($event).

Unit tests updated: sortOrder-related tests removed or updated in ServiceFormDialogComponent spec, CategoryPanelComponent spec, and store specs. New store method tests added for reorderCategories and reorderServices.
npx nx affected -t lint --base=origin/main passes
npx nx affected -t test --base=origin/main passes
npx nx affected -t build --base=origin/main passes
userStories.notes: Use only HTML5 native drag & drop (dragstart/dragover/drop events with event.dataTransfer). No third-party drag & drop libraries. Track the dragged item index in a component signal (draggedIndex = signal<number | null>(null)). On dragstart set draggedIndex. On dragover call event.preventDefault(). On drop: compute new array order, set draggedIndex to null, emit the reordered event. The store reorder methods should first patchState with the new local order (for instant UI feedback), then fire the API calls in sequence. API call failures can be silently ignored for now (no rollback). The sortOrder for each item after reorder is simply its 0-based index in the new array.

userStories.title: Translate all user-facing UI text to Dutch
userStories.description: As a user, I want the application interface to be in Dutch (Nederlands) because that is the primary language of the target market.
acceptanceCriteria: service-list-page.component.html: h1 'Services' → 'Diensten'. Button 'Add Service' → 'Dienst toevoegen'. Delete service dialog: title 'Delete Service' → 'Dienst verwijderen', message translated, confirmLabel 'Delete' → 'Verwijderen'. Delete category dialog: title 'Delete Category' → 'Categorie verwijderen', message translated, confirmLabel 'Delete' → 'Verwijderen'.

service-form-dialog.component.html: dialog title 'Edit Service' → 'Dienst bewerken', 'Add Service' → 'Dienst toevoegen'. Label 'Name' → 'Naam'. Label 'Description' → 'Omschrijving'. Label 'Duration (minutes)' → 'Duur (minuten)'. Label 'Price' → 'Prijs'. Label 'Category' → 'Categorie'. Option 'None' → 'Geen'. Button 'Cancel' → 'Annuleren'. Button 'Save' → 'Opslaan'. Validation message for name → 'Naam is verplicht (max. 150 tekens).'. Validation for description → 'Omschrijving mag maximaal 2000 tekens bevatten.'. Validation for duration → 'Duur is verplicht.'. Validation for price → 'Prijs is verplicht en moet 0 of meer zijn.'.

service-table.component.html: loading text 'Loading services...' → 'Diensten laden...'. Column header 'Name' → 'Naam'. 'Category' → 'Categorie'. 'Duration' → 'Duur'. 'Price' → 'Prijs'. 'Status' → 'Status' (unchanged). 'Actions' → 'Acties'. Badge 'Active' → 'Actief'. Badge 'Inactive' → 'Inactief'. Button title 'Edit service' → 'Dienst bewerken'. Button text 'Edit' → 'Bewerken'. Button title 'Toggle active' → 'Actief/inactief zetten'. Button text 'Deactivate' → 'Deactiveren', 'Activate' → 'Activeren'. Button title 'Delete service' → 'Dienst verwijderen'. Button text 'Delete' → 'Verwijderen'. Empty state text translated to Dutch.

category-panel.component.html: header 'Categories' → 'Categorieën'. Add button '+ Add' → '+ Toevoegen'. Input placeholder 'Category name' → 'Categorienaam'. Button 'Save' → 'Opslaan'. Button 'Cancel' → 'Annuleren'. Loading text 'Loading...' → 'Laden...'. Empty state 'No categories yet.' → 'Nog geen categorieën.'. Button title 'Edit category' → 'Categorie bewerken'. Button text 'Edit' → 'Bewerken'. Button title 'Delete category' → 'Categorie verwijderen'. Button text 'Delete' → 'Verwijderen'.
confirmation-dialog.component.ts (shared/ui): default confirmLabel changed from 'Confirm' to 'Bevestigen'. Default cancelLabel changed from 'Cancel' to 'Annuleren'.
All unit test strings updated where tests assert on rendered text (e.g., button labels, dialog titles).
npx nx affected -t lint --base=origin/main passes
npx nx affected -t test --base=origin/main passes
npx nx affected -t build --base=origin/main passes
userStories.notes: This story MUST be implemented AFTER SCF-027 (drag & drop / sortOrder removal) because SCF-027 changes the form layout and SCF-028 should translate the final state of each file. Template-only changes — no TypeScript logic changes except the default input values in ConfirmationDialogComponent. The delete confirmation messages in service-list-page.component.html: service message → 'Weet je zeker dat je deze dienst wilt verwijderen? Dit kan niet ongedaan worden gemaakt.' Category message → 'Weet je zeker dat je deze categorie wilt verwijderen? Dit kan niet ongedaan worden gemaakt.' All Playwright e2e tests that assert on English text (e.g., 'Add Service' button, 'Services' h1, 'Categories' panel) must be updated to match the Dutch text.

userStories.title: Fix frontend code structure violations
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the frontend code to follow the domain folder conventions defined in CLAUDE.md so that the codebase is maintainable and consistent.
acceptanceCriteria: Scan all files under libs/chairly/src/lib/services/. Any TypeScript interface or type definition files (.ts files containing only interfaces/types) found inside util/ must be moved to models/. Update all imports across the codebase to point to the new location.
Scan all files under libs/chairly/src/lib/services/. Any Angular @Pipe class files found inside util/ must be moved to pipes/. Update all imports across the codebase to point to the new location.

Scan libs/chairly/src/lib/services/feature/. Any component files (.ts, .html, .scss) placed directly inside feature/ (not inside a named subfolder) must be moved to their own subfolder named after the component (e.g., service-list-page/ subfolder for ServiceListPageComponent). Update all imports and references.

Scan all component .ts files under libs/chairly/src/lib/services/ and libs/shared/src/lib/. Any component using inline template: '...' must have its template extracted to a separate .html file using templateUrl: './component-name.component.html'. The extracted .html file must be created alongside the .ts file. The inline template: property must be removed.

If a services.routes.ts (or similarly named routes file) exists inside libs/chairly/src/lib/services/feature/, move it to libs/chairly/src/lib/services/ (the domain root). Update any references to this file in the app routing configuration.
Remove any .gitkeep files from folders that already contain real files. Only .gitkeep files in truly empty folders should remain.
All imports updated — no broken import paths after the moves.
npx nx affected -t lint --base=origin/main passes
npx nx affected -t test --base=origin/main passes
npx nx affected -t build --base=origin/main passes
userStories.notes: These are structural violations found during PR review. When scanning, be thorough — check every .ts file in the services domain and shared lib. The inline template violation is enforced by ESLint (@angular-eslint/component-max-inline-declarations: { template: 0 }) so fixing this will also resolve ESLint errors. After moving files, run a global search for old import paths to ensure nothing is broken.

userStories.title: Fix ESLint failures in CI
userStories.description: As a developer, I want ESLint to pass cleanly in CI so that code quality is enforced automatically on every push.
acceptanceCriteria: Run npx nx affected -t lint --base=origin/main and capture all lint errors.
Fix every lint error found. Do not disable lint rules with eslint-disable comments unless the rule is genuinely inapplicable and documented with a reason.

Common issues to look for: inline template: in components (must use templateUrl:), missing OnPush change detection strategy, use of @Input()/@Output() decorators instead of signal-based input()/model()/OutputEmitterRef, function calls in templates, missing explicit return types on functions, console statements, any types, cross-domain imports violating Sheriff boundaries.
npx nx affected -t lint --base=origin/main exits with code 0 (no errors, no warnings treated as errors).
npx nx affected -t test --base=origin/main passes
npx nx affected -t build --base=origin/main passes
userStories.notes: This story should be implemented AFTER SCF-029 because fixing the structural violations (inline templates, wrong folder placement) will resolve many lint errors. Run lint first to capture a full list, then fix systematically. If @angular-eslint/component-max-inline-declarations is not yet in the ESLint config for the libs, check eslint.config.mjs in libs/chairly/ and libs/shared/ — it should be configured with { template: 0 }.

userStories.title: Configure .NET Aspire to orchestrate API and PostgreSQL
userStories.description: As a developer, I want .NET Aspire to start the API and a PostgreSQL database together so that I can run the full stack locally without manually starting services.
acceptanceCriteria: In Chairly.AppHost/Program.cs: add a PostgreSQL resource using builder.AddPostgres('postgres') and a database resource using .AddDatabase('chairlydb') (or equivalent names matching the connection string convention).
The Chairly.Api project is added to Aspire as a resource using builder.AddProject<Projects.Chairly_Api>('api') and has a reference to the database resource via .WithReference(db).

Chairly.Api reads the connection string from the Aspire-injected connection string named 'chairlydb' (or the name used above). In Chairly.Infrastructure, the DbContext registration uses builder.Configuration.GetConnectionString('chairlydb') or the Aspire-provided environment variable.
Running dotnet run --project src/backend/Chairly.AppHost starts both the API and the PostgreSQL container without errors.
The Aspire dashboard shows both the 'api' and 'postgres' resources as healthy.
dotnet build src/backend/Chairly.slnx passes
dotnet test src/backend/Chairly.slnx passes
userStories.notes: Use the Aspire hosting packages: Aspire.Hosting.PostgreSQL for PostgreSQL. Add the NuGet package Aspire.Hosting.PostgreSQL to Chairly.AppHost if not present. For the API to read the injected connection string, ensure Chairly.Api references Aspire.ServiceDefaults or uses builder.AddServiceDefaults() in Program.cs. The connection string name used in AddDatabase must match GetConnectionString() call in the Infrastructure layer. Check existing DbContext registration in Chairly.Infrastructure for the current connection string name.

userStories.title: Create admin dashboard shell with navigation
userStories.description: As a user, I want the application to have a consistent admin dashboard layout with a sidebar navigation menu so that I can navigate between features easily and the application looks professional.
acceptanceCriteria: Create a shared shell/layout component at libs/shared/src/lib/ui/shell/ named ShellComponent (selector: chairly-shell). It must use OnPush change detection and templateUrl.
ShellComponent renders a sidebar navigation on the left and a <router-outlet> content area on the right. The sidebar must include a navigation link for 'Diensten' (Services) pointing to /diensten.

The sidebar must be styled using Tailwind CSS v4 classes to look like a professional admin dashboard: dark sidebar background (e.g. bg-gray-900 or bg-slate-800), white text, hover states on nav items, active link highlighting.
The main app routing in apps/chairly/src/app/app.routes.ts wraps all feature routes inside the ShellComponent layout. The /diensten route loads the services domain routes lazily.
The services domain route is updated: the URL path is 'diensten' (Dutch) instead of 'services' if it currently uses English. The lazy-loaded route still points to the services domain routes file.
The root path '/' redirects to '/diensten'.
ShellComponent is exported from libs/shared/src/lib/index.ts (or equivalent barrel file).
Existing unit tests for the app still pass. Update any route-related tests to use the new paths.
npx nx affected -t lint --base=origin/main passes
npx nx affected -t test --base=origin/main passes
npx nx affected -t build --base=origin/main passes
userStories.notes: The shell should be minimal but polished — a vertical sidebar on the left (~240px wide) with the app name/logo at the top, navigation links in the middle, and the router-outlet taking up the remaining space. Use RouterLink and RouterLinkActive for navigation. The active route class should be applied via routerLinkActive='bg-gray-700' (or similar). Keep the shell component simple — no complex state management needed. The sidebar nav items can be hardcoded for now (only 'Diensten' needed). All user-facing text in the shell must be in Dutch.

userStories.title: Fix modal/dialog positioning — center on screen
userStories.description: As a user, I want popup dialogs to always appear centered on my screen so that they are easy to see and interact with.
acceptanceCriteria: Identify all dialog/modal components in the codebase (e.g., ServiceFormDialogComponent, ConfirmationDialogComponent in shared/ui, and any others).
Each dialog must render a full-screen overlay (position: fixed, inset-0, z-50) with a semi-transparent background (bg-black/50 or equivalent).
The dialog box itself must be centered both horizontally and vertically within the overlay using Tailwind classes: flex items-center justify-center on the overlay element.
The dialog box must have a maximum width (e.g., max-w-lg or max-w-md) and be responsive — on small screens it should not overflow horizontally.
When a dialog is open, the page behind it must not be scrollable (overflow-hidden on body, or equivalent).
Opening any dialog/popup in the application shows it centered on the screen at all viewport sizes.
npx nx affected -t lint --base=origin/main passes
npx nx affected -t test --base=origin/main passes
npx nx affected -t build --base=origin/main passes
userStories.notes: The current issue is that the popup opens in the top-left corner of the screen. This is typically caused by the overlay using position: absolute or by missing flex centering classes. The fix is to ensure the overlay element has: class='fixed inset-0 z-50 flex items-center justify-center bg-black/50' and the inner dialog box has: class='bg-white rounded-lg shadow-xl w-full max-w-lg mx-4'. Check both ServiceFormDialogComponent and ConfirmationDialogComponent (and any others). Also check if the dialog is rendered inside a positioned parent — if so, use a CDK portal or move the dialog rendering to the document body level.

userStories.title: Add Playwright E2E tests for the services feature
userStories.description: As a developer, I want comprehensive E2E tests for the services management feature so that regressions are caught automatically.
acceptanceCriteria: Create E2E test file at apps/chairly-e2e/src/services.spec.ts.
Test: navigating to /diensten shows the 'Diensten' page heading and the services table.
Test: clicking 'Dienst toevoegen' opens a centered dialog with the service form fields (Naam, Omschrijving, Duur, Prijs, Categorie).
Test: filling in the form and clicking 'Opslaan' calls the API and shows the new service in the table (mock or intercept the API response).
Test: clicking 'Bewerken' on a service row opens the form dialog pre-filled with that service's data.
Test: clicking 'Verwijderen' on a service row shows the confirmation dialog; clicking 'Verwijderen' in the dialog removes the service from the table.
Test: the category panel shows the 'Categorieën' header and a list of categories.
Test: clicking '+ Toevoegen' in the category panel shows an input field for a new category name.
All tests must pass with npx nx e2e chairly-e2e.
Tests use page.route() to intercept and mock API calls — do not depend on a running backend.
npx nx affected -t lint --base=origin/main passes
userStories.notes: Use Playwright's page.route() to intercept API calls and return mock data. This way the tests are fast and deterministic without needing a real backend. The base URL for the dev server is configured in playwright.config.ts — check that it points to the correct port (typically 4200). Use data-testid attributes sparingly — prefer role-based or text-based selectors (getByRole, getByText) following Playwright best practices. The tests must run in CI with the app served statically or with a dev server started by Playwright's webServer config.

userStories.title: Fix Nx Daemon EPIPE error on Windows
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the Nx Daemon to work reliably (or be disabled cleanly) so that I don't see 'Nx Daemon is disabled' warnings and file changes are detected correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: Investigate the root cause of the EPIPE error in the Nx daemon log (write EPIPE on the socket connection).

If the issue is Windows-specific and cannot be fixed reliably, disable the daemon by setting 'useDaemonProcess': false in nx.json. This prevents misleading 'daemon disabled' warnings and makes Nx behavior predictable.
If a config fix is possible (e.g., increasing socket timeout, using a different IPC mechanism), apply it instead of disabling.
After the fix, running any nx command (e.g., npx nx show projects) does not print 'Nx Daemon is disabled' or EPIPE errors.
npx nx affected -t lint --base=origin/main passes
npx nx affected -t build --base=origin/main passes
userStories.notes: The EPIPE error occurs because the Nx daemon socket connection is broken mid-request on Windows (named pipe issue). The simplest fix is to add 'useDaemonProcess': false to the root nx.json. This disables the daemon entirely and makes Nx run in-process, which is slower but reliable. Check nx.json at src/frontend/chairly/nx.json — add the useDaemonProcess property. If the daemon is already configured but broken, also run 'npx nx daemon --stop' before the fix to ensure a clean state.
